Amended Ntc Of Hrng On Petn, Sierra Pacific Insur. Co'S Petn For Writ Of Administrative Mandamus, Calendar Motion: Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Jan-17-2002 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEAR: Robert A. Lewis, Esq. & Christine Banks, Esq. 393-2000 for petitioner & moving party; Howard L. Pearlman, Esq. 956-1900 for respondent & opposing party.
RULING: The Court announced its tentative ruling as follows: Grant motion to remand for calculation of the variance. Hall report to be considered. Insurance Commissioner to accept additional briefing re method of computing constitutional variance. Additional necessary evidence to be permitted.
Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling.

Amended Ntc Of Hrng On Petn, Sierra Pacific Insur. Co'S Petn For Writ Of Administrative Mandamus, Calendar Motion: Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Dec-19-2001 Continued To Jan-17-02 At 03:30 P.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: APPEAR: Robert A. Lewis, Esq. & Christine Banks, Esq. 393-2000 for petitioner & moving party; Howard L. Pearlman, Esq. 956-1900 for respondent & opposing party.
RULING: The Court announced its tentative ruling as follows:
(1)Writ denied re motion for stay pending trial of action against United Chinese Agency.
(2)Writ denied re claim of improper calculation of roll back. 1)understated loss reserve denied. Hall report properly held inadmissible. 2)defacto rate reduction, no evidence of actual rate reduction. 3)calculation of ULAE and ALAE re auto insurance, weight of evidence supports finding of Commissioner. 4)calculation of CIGA, weight of evidence of Yen testimony outweighs testimony of Friese.
(3)Claim for regulatory variance denied. Failure to show plan to restore financial condition and failure to show plan to reduce rates. Claim that constitutional right of equal protection for qualification for regulation variance denied - failure to establish through weight of evidence others who are similarly situated.
(4)As to the claim of improper denial of constitutional variance grant. Weight of evidence supports a denial of the rights to a constitutional variance.
Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling. Mr. Lewis to prepare order. In addition, the Court continues the matter re question on remedy for further hearing to /1/17/02 at 3:30pm. Mr. Pearlman to submit 10 page brief by 01/04; Mr. Lewis 10 pages by 01/11.
